model_narrative: We must procure a fixed total quantity of a commodity from a set of vendors. Each vendor offers one or more deals, where a deal is a price segment with its own fixed setup cost, unit price, and quantity limits. For each available vendor-segment deal we decide whether to select it and how much to purchase on it. Choosing a deal incurs its setup cost, and every purchased unit is charged at that deal's unit price. The objective is to minimize the total procurement cost, which combines the setup costs of the selected deals and the unit purchase costs of the quantities bought.

sets.name: v
sets.doc: the vendors that can supply the commodity

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the price segments a vendor may offer, given as ascending tier numbers; a higher segment number is a higher-volume tier

sets.name: vs
sets.doc: the set of valid vendor-segment deals that are actually offered; a vendor may offer several segments and only the pairs listed here are available to choose

params.name: req
params.doc: the total quantity of the commodity that must be procured, in units

params.name: setup
params.doc: the fixed setup cost charged in dollars for selecting a given vendor-segment deal, incurred once if that deal is chosen

params.name: price
params.doc: the unit purchase price in dollars per unit for a given vendor-segment deal

params.name: qmin
params.doc: the minimum quantity in units that must be purchased on a vendor-segment deal if that deal is selected

params.name: qmax
params.doc: the maximum quantity in units that may be purchased on a vendor-segment deal

vars.name: c
vars.doc: the total procurement cost over all chosen deals, in dollars

vars.name: pl
vars.doc: the quantity in units purchased on each vendor-segment deal

vars.name: plb
vars.doc: the binary decision for each vendor-segment deal, equal to one if that deal is selected and zero otherwise

constraints.name: req
constraints.intent: The total quantity purchased across all available vendor-segment deals must exactly meet the required procurement amount. Add up the quantity bought on every deal and set that sum equal to the total requirement.

constraints.name: c
constraints.intent: The total procurement cost accounts for both the per-unit purchase charges and the fixed setup charges. For each available deal, charge its unit price on every unit purchased and charge its setup cost once if that deal is selected. Set the total cost equal to the sum of these amounts over all deals.

constraints.name: pl[v,
constraints.intent: Each deal carries a minimum purchase quantity that applies only when the deal is selected. For every available vendor-segment deal, the quantity purchased on it must be at least its minimum quantity when the deal is chosen, and may be nothing when it is not chosen.

constraints.name: pl[v,
constraints.intent: Each deal carries a maximum purchase quantity that applies only when the deal is selected. For every available vendor-segment deal, the quantity purchased on it must not exceed its maximum quantity when the deal is chosen, and must be nothing when it is not chosen.

constraints.name: plb[v,
constraints.intent: A vendor's segments are alternatives, so at most one deal may be accepted from any single vendor. For each vendor, the number of selected deals among that vendor's available segments must be no more than one.

query: Procurement wants to keep the supplier base tight — let's see what happens if we award deals to no more than two vendors in total.
expected_pyomo: model.deal_count = Constraint(expr=sum(model.plb[vs] for vs in model.vs) <= 2)

query: Supplier relations is pushing to keep vendor b in the mix for strategic reasons. If we require at least one awarded deal with vendor b, how does the cost shift?
expected_pyomo: model.use_b = Constraint(expr=sum(model.plb['b', s] for s in model.s if ('b', s) in model.vs) >= 1)

query: Only three suppliers in the award feels thin for resilience. Wouldn't it be wiser to spread across at least four vendors? What's the model seeing that I'm not?
expected_pyomo: model.min_vendors = Constraint(expr=sum(model.plb[vs] for vs in model.vs) >= 4)
